target.title: fix(followup): stop model-fallback retries duplicating queued user messages and assistant error stubs
target.body:
## Summary

- Followup queue + model fallback: latch queued user-message persistence so only the first fallback candidate writes the queued user message to the session JSONL (issue body case).
- Followup queue + model fallback: suppress the `[assistant turn failed before producing content]` stub on every fallback candidate after the first, so the JSONL never gains consecutive assistant-error entries (issue follow-up comment, observed in steer mode).
- Add `suppressAssistantErrorPersistence` to the session tool-result guard and wrapper; plumb through pi-embedded-runner params and the embedded attempt / overload loop.
- Regression tests: contract-level (`runEmbeddedPiAgent` receives the right suppress flags on each fallback candidate) and behavior-level (real `SessionManager` + guard cascade ends with exactly `[user, assistant]` and zero consecutive same-role pairs).

## Why

With `messages.queue.mode: \"followup\"`, `runWithModelFallback` invokes its `run` callback once per candidate model. Each invocation re-passed `queued.transcriptPrompt` into `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, and the embedded session manager re-appended the queued user message; on top of that, every failed candidate wrote an `[assistant turn failed before producing content]` stub to the JSONL. The session ended up with consecutive same-role entries (4× user in followup mode, 4× assistant in steer mode), the next request to Anthropic was rejected for that reason, OpenRouter masked the upstream 400 as a 500, and the failover loop ran again — death spiral, ~4KB session growth per cycle, unrecoverable without manual session deletion.

The fix is two latches in `createFollowupRunner`'s `runWithModelFallback` `run:` callback:

1. `queuedUserMessagePersistedAcrossFallback` — flipped by `onUserMessagePersisted`. ORed into `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` so the second+ candidate skips re-appending the queued user message.
2. `fallbackCandidateAttemptIndex` — counter; passes `suppressAssistantErrorPersistence: true` on every candidate after the first. The session tool-result guard then skips assistant messages with `stopReason === \"error\"`.

CLI runner branch is untouched: it does not accept `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence`/`suppressAssistantErrorPersistence` and owns its own transcript management. Scope limited to the embedded path described in the issue (OpenRouter `openai-completions`, four anthropic fallback models).

Refs: #83404

## Verification

Local:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
```

Output:

```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  76 passed (76)
   Duration  4.94s
```

Build:

```
pnpm build
```

passes (required because `params.ts` and the guard wrapper grew a new public field).

Behavior addressed: queued followup user message and per-candidate assistant error stub both duplicated in session JSONL on model-fallback retry.

Real environment tested: behavior-level integration through the real `SessionManager` + `installSessionToolResultGuard` (no live OpenRouter run). The integration test in `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts` simulates a four-candidate fallback cascade end-to-end and asserts the resulting session contains exactly `[user, assistant]` with zero consecutive same-role pairs — i.e. the exact session-JSONL invariant that was violated in the issue report.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts -t "four-candidate followup fallback cascade"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "queued user message idempotency across fallback"
```

Evidence after fix: integration cascade asserts `roles === [\"user\", \"assistant\"]` and `consecutiveSameRolePairs === 0` after four simulated failed fallback candidates. Contract tests on `runEmbeddedPiAgent` confirm `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` is `true` on candidate 2+ once `onUserMessagePersisted` has fired, and `suppressAssistantErrorPersistence` is `true` on every candidate after the first.

Observed result after fix: all 76 tests pass; behavior-level integration shows exactly one user entry and one assistant-error entry persist for a four-candidate failure cascade.

What was not tested: a real OpenRouter + Anthropic-fallback live failover run on a real channel. Reporter's environment (`messages.queue.mode: \"followup\"`, OpenRouter `openai-completions` provider, four anthropic fallback models) was reproduced through the real session-persistence stack but not against a live provider. Maintainer with provider credentials should verify on the reported configuration before merge.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts`
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm build`
- [ ] live OpenRouter + followup-mode + provoked upstream 500 (maintainer with credentials)
target.author: yetval
